category_title: Adolescent student with blindness and selective mutism who uses voice-output devices for communication
scenario_title: Introduction of a new voice-output device
minimal_context: Sally and her instructor are sitting at a table during one-on-one instruction to use a second voice-output device.

dialogue.speaker: tutor
dialogue.message: Sally, I have a second voice-output device for you today. It is the big button communicator.

dialogue.speaker: student
dialogue.message: (Remains silent, pushes the device away from her and refuses to use it.)

dialogue.speaker: tutor
dialogue.message: Let's try this again. I am putting the device back on the table in front of you.

dialogue.speaker: student
dialogue.message: (Sits silently and continues to refuse to use the device.)

dialogue.speaker: tutor
dialogue.message: Please use the buttons to communicate a choice.

dialogue.speaker: student
dialogue.message: (Places her arm across all four buttons, rather than use the device appropriately to communicate a choice.)

dialogue_combined:
TUTOR: Sally, I have a second voice-output device for you today. It is the big button communicator.

STUDENT: (Remains silent, pushes the device away from her and refuses to use it.)

TUTOR: Let's try this again. I am putting the device back on the table in front of you.

STUDENT: (Sits silently and continues to refuse to use the device.)

TUTOR: Please use the buttons to communicate a choice.

STUDENT: (Places her arm across all four buttons, rather than use the device appropriately to communicate a choice.)

task.content: Introduction of a second voice-output device

context.social_setting: One-on-one instruction with the first author

challenging_behavior_trigger.condition: When she was first presented with a second voice-output device, it was described to her as 'the big button communicator.'
challenging_behavior_trigger.observable_behavior: She pushed the device away from her and refused to use it.

behavior_patterns.observable_behavior: she placed her arm across all four buttons, rather than use the device appropriately to communicate a choice.
